pay for a portion of the cost.
This water power company has
not paid cent for single fran
chise granted it in this city. Those
franchises are now worth a million
at a low estimate.


Now and then they make a petty
agreement such as repairing a
bridge-and then spend months
working with only the means they
can use with the city council to
secure a cancellation of even these
small considerations.


Both companies, traction and
water power, should in future bid
for franchises. Their past agree
ments they should be forced to
keep.


The city should not vote one cent
to build the Howard street bridge.


The people should be at the city
hall tonight to see that their repre-
sentatives do their duty.


The following is a copy of the
letter sent to the city council by
Graves & Graves, attorneys for the
Spokane Traction company, offer
ing to give to the city of Spokane
$4,000 for franchise across the
Howard street bridge:


1--WE have this day filed a pro-
posed franchise for the Spokane
Traction company for street rail-
